summary.headline: ADM reports Q2 2025 results with adjusted EPS of $0.93, down 10% year-over-year.
summary.narrative_summary: Archer-Daniels-Midland Company (ADM) reported second quarter 2025 financial results, showing adjusted net earnings of $452 million and adjusted EPS of $0.93, down 10% from the prior year. The company's total segment operating profit was $830 million, also down 10% year-over-year. The company tightened its full-year 2025 adjusted EPS guidance to approximately $4.00 per share, citing improved margins expected to benefit the fourth quarter. Segment performance varied, with Ag Services and Oilseeds down 17%, Carbohydrate Solutions down 6%, and Nutrition up 5%.

drivers: decrease in volumes and margins due to trade policy uncertainty
concerns: lower vegetable oil demand due to biofuel and trade policy uncertainty

drivers: improved global wheat milling margins and volumes
concerns: lower international S S volumes and margins
lower margins in Specialty Ingredients due to Decatur East plant impacts

extraction_notes: Numbers extracted from Exhibit 99.1; cover sheet only had Item references. Guidance changes noted but no specific numerical guidance provided.
